mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.25% and remains elevated for at least one month, equity risk premiums expand due to higher discount rates and reduced refinancing capacity. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.25% for 1+ months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate of 0.3 percentage points or greater signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. This negative demand shock produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations contract.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.3pp or more in one month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE monthly changes and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer and construction demand. When both series decline month-over-month simultaneously, aggregate demand weakens, reducing forward earnings growth expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline, or the interaction effect (joint decline) does not explain incremental variance beyond individual series (R² increase < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real-side economic capacity utilization. A sustained decline in INDPRO over two consecutive months indicates supply-side weakness and reduced profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice earnings quality downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO declines for 2+ consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL and the federal funds rate jointly determine real monetary conditions. When FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL inflation rate by more than 2 percentage points, real rates are restrictive, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: restrictive real rate regime with monetary tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where real rates exceed 2.0pp, or the interaction term does not explain incremental variance beyond individual series (R² increase < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) is an always-on trend signal. Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS above 150,000 persons indicates robust labor demand and consumer income growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through higher expected corporate revenues.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods with PAYEMS growth > 150k month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead consumer spending and construction employment. When HOUST rises above 1,400 thousand units annualized, forward housing demand signals robust aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where HOUST > 1400, or the correlation between HOUST levels and 5-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure supply-side capacity and labor utilization. When both series grow month-over-month simultaneously, productive capacity expansion signals sustained profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where both INDPRO and PAYEMS increase, or the interaction effect does not explain incremental variance beyond individual series (R² increase < 0.02).
